title: गीत गोविन्द — प्रथम अष्टपदी
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गीत गोविन्द — प्रथम अष्टपदी ॥
(श्री जयदेव कृत)

॥ प्रथम सर्ग — सामोद-दामोदरम् ॥

मेघैर्मेदुरमम्बरं वनभुवः श्यामास्तमालद्रुमै-
र्नक्तं भीरुरयं त्वमेव तदिमां राधे गृहं प्रापय।
इत्थं नन्दनिदेशतश्चलितयोः प्रत्यध्वकुञ्जद्रुमं
राधामाधवयोर्जयन्ति यमुनाकूले रहःकेलयः॥ १॥

॥ अष्टपदी — प्रलयपयोधिजले ॥

प्रलयपयोधिजले धृतवानसि वेदं
विहितवहित्रचरित्रमखेदम्।
केशव धृतमीनशरीर जय जगदीश हरे॥ ध्रुवम् ॥

क्षितिरतिविपुलतरे तव तिष्ठति पृष्ठे
धरणिधरणकिणचक्रगरिष्ठे।
केशव धृतकच्छपरूप जय जगदीश हरे॥ १॥

वसति दशनशिखरे धरणी तव लग्ना
शशिनि कलङ्ककलेव निमग्ना।
केशव धृतसूकररूप जय जगदीश हरे॥ २॥

तव करकमलवरे नखमद्भुतशृङ्गं
दलितहिरण्यकशिपुतनुभृङ्गम्।
केशव धृतनरहरिरूप जय जगदीश हरे॥ ३॥

छलयसि विक्रमणे बलिमद्भुतवामन
पदनखनीरजनितजनपावन।
केशव धृतवामनरूप जय जगदीश हरे॥ ४॥

क्षत्रियरुधिरमये जगदपगतपापं
स्नपयसि पयसि शमितभवतापम्।
केशव धृतभृगुपतिरूप जय जगदीश हरे॥ ५॥

वितरसि दिक्षु रणे दिक्पतिकमनीयं
दशमुखमौलिबलिं रमणीयम्।
केशव धृतरामशरीर जय जगदीश हरे॥ ६॥

वहसि वपुषि विशदे वसनं जलदाभं
हलहतिभीतिमिलितयमुनाभम्।
केशव धृतहलधररूप जय जगदीश हरे॥ ७॥

निन्दसि यज्ञविधेरहह श्रुतिजातं
सदयहृदयदर्शितपशुघातम्।
केशव धृतबुद्धशरीर जय जगदीश हरे॥ ८॥

म्लेच्छनिवहनिधने कलयसि करवालं
धूमकेतुमिव किमपि करालम्।
केशव धृतकल्किशरीर जय जगदीश हरे॥ ९॥

श्रीजयदेवकवेरिदमुदितमुदारं
श्रृणु सुखदं शुभदं भवसारम्।
केशव धृतदशविधरूप जय जगदीश हरे॥ १०॥

॥ इति श्री जयदेव कृत गीत गोविन्द प्रथम अष्टपदी सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Gita Govinda — Prathama Ashtapadi ||
(Shri Jayadeva Krit)

|| Prathama Sarga — Samoda-Damodaram ||

Meghair-meduram-ambaram vanabhuvah shyamas-tamala-drumai-
naktam bhirur-ayam tvam-eva tad-imam Radhe griham prapaya |
Ittham Nanda-nideshatas-chalitayoh praty-adhva-kunja-drumam
Radha-Madhavayoh jayanti Yamuna-kule rahah-kelayah || 1 ||

|| Ashtapadi — Pralaya-payodhi-jale ||

Pralaya-payodhi-jale dhritavan-asi Vedam
vihita-vahitra-charitram-akhedam |
Keshava dhrita-meena-sharira jaya Jagadisha Hare || Dhruvam ||

Kshitir-ati-vipulatare tava tishthati prishthe
dharani-dharana-kina-chakra-garishthe |
Keshava dhrita-kachchhapa-rupa jaya Jagadisha Hare || 1 ||

Vasati dashana-shikhare dharani tava lagna
shashini kalanka-kaleva nimagna |
Keshava dhrita-sukara-rupa jaya Jagadisha Hare || 2 ||

Tava kara-kamala-vare nakham-adbhuta-shringam
dalita-Hiranyakashipu-tanu-bhringam |
Keshava dhrita-Narahari-rupa jaya Jagadisha Hare || 3 ||

Chhalayasi Vikramane Balim-adbhuta-Vamana
pada-nakha-neeraja-nita-jana-pavana |
Keshava dhrita-Vamana-rupa jaya Jagadisha Hare || 4 ||

Kshatriya-rudhira-maye jagad-apagata-papam
snapayasi payasi shamita-bhava-tapam |
Keshava dhrita-Bhrigupati-rupa jaya Jagadisha Hare || 5 ||

Vitarasi dikshu rane dikpati-kamaniyam
Dasha-mukha-mauli-balim ramaniyam |
Keshava dhrita-Rama-sharira jaya Jagadisha Hare || 6 ||

Vahasi vapushi vishade vasanam jaladabham
hala-hati-bhiti-milita-Yamunabham |
Keshava dhrita-Haladhara-rupa jaya Jagadisha Hare || 7 ||

Nindasi yajna-vidher-ahaha shruti-jatam
sadaya-hridaya-darshita-pashu-ghatam |
Keshava dhrita-Buddha-sharira jaya Jagadisha Hare || 8 ||

Mlechchha-nivaha-nidhane kalayasi karavalam
Dhuma-ketum-iva kimapi karalam |
Keshava dhrita-Kalki-sharira jaya Jagadisha Hare || 9 ||

Shri-Jayadeva-kaver-idam-uditam-udaram
shrinu sukhadam shubhadam bhava-saram |
Keshava dhrita-dasha-vidha-rupa jaya Jagadisha Hare || 10 ||

|| Iti Shri Jayadeva Krit Gita Govinda Prathama Ashtapadi Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
मेघों से मन्दिर आकाश, तमाल वृक्षों से श्याम वनभूमि — रात्रि में भयभीत यह (राधा) — 'तुम्हीं इसे घर पहुँचाओ, राधे!' — ऐसा नन्द के आदेश से चलती हुई राधा-माधव की यमुनातट पर रहस्य-केलियाँ जयवन्ती हों!॥ १॥

प्रलय के जल में वेद को धारण किया — हे केशव, मीन शरीर धारी, जय जगदीश हरे!॥

अत्यन्त विपुल पृथ्वी आपकी पीठ पर स्थित है — हे केशव, कच्छप (कूर्म) रूप धारी, जय जगदीश हरे!॥ १॥

आपके दाँतों की नोक पर पृथ्वी लगी है जैसे चन्द्रमा में कलंक — हे केशव, वराह रूप धारी, जय जगदीश हरे!॥ २॥

आपके करकमल में अद्भुत नखश्रृंग से हिरण्यकशिपु का शरीररूपी भ्रमर विदलित हुआ — हे केशव, नरहरि रूप धारी, जय जगदीश हरे!॥ ३॥

बलि को विक्रमण में छलते हैं, अद्भुत वामन — पदनख से निकले जल से जन पावन — हे केशव, वामन रूप धारी, जय जगदीश हरे!॥ ४॥

क्षत्रिय-रुधिर से जगत् निष्पाप किया, भवताप शमित किया — हे केशव, भृगुपति (परशुराम) रूप धारी, जय जगदीश हरे!॥ ५॥

दिशाओं में रण में दिक्पतियों को कमनीय, दशमुख (रावण) का मौलिबलि रमणीय — हे केशव, राम शरीर धारी, जय जगदीश हरे!॥ ६॥

विशद शरीर पर जलदसम वसन धारे, हल से भयभीत यमुना सम — हे केशव, हलधर (बलराम) रूप धारी, जय जगदीश हरे!॥ ७॥

यज्ञ में पशुघात देख सदय हृदय से निन्दा की — हे केशव, बुद्ध शरीर धारी, जय जगदीश हरे!॥ ८॥

म्लेच्छों के नाश हेतु करवाल (तलवार) धारण करते — धूमकेतु सम कराल — हे केशव, कल्कि शरीर धारी, जय जगदीश हरे!॥ ९॥

श्री जयदेव कवि का यह उदित, उदार, सुखद, शुभद, भवसार गीत सुनो — हे केशव, दशविध रूप धारी, जय जगदीश हरे!॥ १०॥
benefits: Invokes all ten avatars of Lord Vishnu simultaneously
Invokes the combined blessings of dashavatara
One of the most melodious and powerful devotional compositions
Purifies the heart through divine love poetry
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins through remembrance of the lord's leelas
Invokes the blessing of bhakti and spiritual ecstasy
description: Gita Govinda - Ashtapadi 1 (गीत गोविन्द — प्रथम अष्टपदी) is a sacred prayer dedicated to Lord Krishna. This revered text originates from Jayadeva - Gita Govinda (Sarga 1). Devotees recite this prayer to invoke all ten avatars of Lord Vishnu simultaneously and bestow bhakti and spiritual ecstasy. It is also said to remove all sins through remembrance of the Lord's leelas. For best results, devotees chant this on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Janmashtami and Holi. At 11 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 10 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Gita Govinda - Ashtapadi 1 in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Gita Govinda Ashtapadi 1 by Jayadeva in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. The famous Dashavatara song Pralaya Payodhi Jale.

seoFaqs.question: What is Gita Govinda Ashtapadi 1 (Pralaya Payodhi Jale)?
seoFaqs.answer: Gita Govinda Ashtapadi 1 — Pralaya Payodhi Jale — is the celebrated opening Dashavatara Stotra of Jayadeva's Gita Govinda, a 12th-century Sanskrit kavya (court epic) on the divine love of Radha and Krishna composed at the court of King Lakshmana Sena of Bengal (or, by tradition, in the village of Kenduli in present-day Odisha). The Gita Govinda is structured into twelve sargas (cantos) containing twenty-four ashtapadis (eight-line songs); the first ashtapadi of Sarga 1 (Samoda-Damodaram) is the famous Dashavatara Stotra praising the ten incarnations of Vishnu — Matsya, Kurma, Varaha, Narahari (Narasimha), Vamana, Bhrigupati (Parashurama), Rama, Haladhara (Balarama), Buddha and Kalki — each verse ending in the refrain Keshava dhrita-X-sharira jaya Jagadisha Hare. It is preceded by the famous opening verse Meghair-meduram-ambaram describing Nanda entrusting Radha to Krishna at twilight on the Yamuna. (Source: Jayadeva, Gita Govinda Sarga 1.)

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Gita Govinda and when?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gita Govinda was composed by Sri Jayadeva (c. 12th century CE), a Vaishnava poet-saint and one of the pancha-sakha tradition. Tradition records two competing biographies: in Bengal, Jayadeva is associated with the court of King Lakshmana Sena of Gauda (modern West Bengal) along with the four other gems Govardhana, Sarana, Umapati and Dhoyi; in Odisha, he is the bhakta-poet of Kenduli Sasan village near Puri, husband of Padmavati the temple dancer, whose composition was so beloved of Lord Jagannatha that the deity is said to have completed an unfinished verse in Jayadeva's hand (the celebrated dehi-pada-pallavam-udaram episode). The Gita Govinda is preserved in the Jagannatha temple seva-paddhati at Puri, where the ashtapadis are sung nightly during the Badasinghara besha and have been since the 13th century. (Sources: Madhya-yuga Vaishnava tradition; Jagannatha Mandir Puri seva-paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the ten avatars praised in the Pralaya Payodhi Jale ashtapadi?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dashavatara Stotra praises the ten Vishnu avatars in classical sequence: (1) Matsya — the fish who saved the Vedas in the deluge waters (pralaya-payodhi), (2) Kurma — the tortoise whose back held Mount Mandara during Samudra Manthan (kachchhapa-rupa), (3) Varaha — the boar who lifted the earth on his tusk (sukara-rupa), (4) Narasimha — the man-lion who tore Hiranyakashipu (narahari-rupa), (5) Vamana — the dwarf-Brahmin who measured Bali's three worlds (vamana-rupa), (6) Parashurama — the Bhrigu-axe-bearer who cleansed the earth (Bhrigupati-rupa), (7) Rama — the Ayodhya prince who slew Ravana (Rama-sharira), (8) Balarama — the plough-bearer who diverted the Yamuna (haladhara-rupa), (9) Buddha — the compassionate one who condemned animal sacrifice (Buddha-sharira), and (10) Kalki — the future avatara on a horse with a sword to destroy the mlechchhas (Kalki-sharira). The eleventh verse signs off with Sri-Jayadeva-kaver-idam — Jayadeva's poet's signature. (Source: Gita Govinda Sarga 1, Ashtapadi 1.)

seoFaqs.question: When should Gita Govinda Ashtapadi 1 be recited or sung?
seoFaqs.answer: The first ashtapadi of the Gita Govinda — Pralaya Payodhi Jale — is most powerful on Janmashtami (Krishna's birth), Radha Ashtami (Bhadrapada Shukla Ashtami — Radha's birthday), Holi / Dol Purnima (the spring festival of divine love), Sharad Purnima (the Maha-rasa night), Vaikuntha Ekadashi, the Brahmotsavam of Jagannatha Puri, and Wednesday (Budhvar) and Friday (Shukravar) — the days of Krishna and Radha. At the Sri Jagannatha Mandir of Puri, the ashtapadis form the nightly Badasinghara seva and are sung in the temple's traditional ragas. In South Indian bhajana paddhati (the Sampradaya Bhajana of Marudhanallur Sadguru Swami and Bodhendra Saraswati), the ashtapadis are central to the morning and evening bhajana sessions. Solo singers in Odissi, Bharatanatyam and Carnatic concerts perform Pralaya Payodhi Jale in ragas like Kalyani, Suruti and Yamunakalyani. (Sources: Jagannatha Puri tradition; Sampradaya Bhajana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does reciting Gita Govinda Ashtapadi 1 grant?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following blessings to sincere recitation or hearing of Pralaya Payodhi Jale: (1) the merit of worshipping all ten Vishnu avatars simultaneously in a single composition — equivalent to performing ten separate avatara pujas, (2) protection through every cosmic age — Matsya for floods, Varaha for stability, Narasimha for fearless protection, Rama for dharma, Krishna for prema, Kalki for liberation from kali-yuga, (3) prema-bhakti — awakening of divine love through the Shringara rasa of the surrounding Radha-Krishna kavya, (4) liberation from sins through smarana of the Lord's leelas, (5) sukhadam, shubhadam, bhava-saram — happiness, auspiciousness and the essence of worldly experience as promised by Jayadeva himself in verse 11, (6) admission into the Vaishnava acharya parampara (Madhva, Nimbarka, Vallabha, Chaitanya all wrote commentaries on it), and (7) liberation through the unique Madhura-bhava (the lover-mood) of bhakti. (Sources: Jayadeva phala-shruti; Vaishnava acharya commentaries.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between the Dashavatara Stotra and Gita Govinda Ashtapadi 1?
seoFaqs.answer: The two are closely related but not identical. The Dashavatara Stotra is the generic devotional category of any hymn praising the ten avataras — many exist (the Vamana Purana version, the Garuda Purana version, Vedanta Desika's Dashavatara Stotra, the Madhva Dashavatara Stuti, etc.). Gita Govinda Ashtapadi 1 is one specific Dashavatara Stotra — the Pralaya Payodhi Jale composition — embedded as the opening ashtapadi of Jayadeva's Gita Govinda, immediately following the Meghair-meduram-ambaram opening verse of Sarga 1. It is uniquely set in the ashtapadi (eight-couplet) musical form with a recurring refrain Keshava dhrita-X-sharira jaya Jagadisha Hare and concludes with a poet's signature verse mentioning Jayadeva by name. It is the most widely sung Dashavatara Stotra in Vaishnava temples worldwide. Other Dashavatara Stotras are recited as path; this one is sung as a kirtana with raga and tala. (Source: Vaishnava stotra tradition.)
